summary: Frontend-only testing for Dmitry Gofman static personal-brand landing page. All 14 requested test scenarios pass: page renders with #0A0A0A bg, hero (headline 'Engineer. Builder. Seeker.', subheadline, microcopy, both CTAs with correct hrefs), sticky nav with 6 testid'd anchor links, Worldview (4 pillars: Attention/Alignment/Iteration/Response), Engineering section (4 cards card-rd/electromechanical/manufacturing/integration with correct titles), bg-toggle aria-expanded false->true, Builder/Human/Vision sections (6 qualities, 6 vision items), Contact links (mailto, linkedin.com/in/dima-gofman, github.com/dmitrygofman), footer with year 2026, reveal animations IntersectionObserver working (2 visible on load, 3 after scroll), smooth scroll-to-section works, and mobile (375px) nav-toggle visible + toggles open class and aria-expanded. No console errors.

issues: Programmatic Playwright hover() did not produce a measurable transform on card-rd (computed transform stayed identity matrix). The CSS rule appears defined but real-browser hover should be visually verified — likely a Playwright-specific :hover synthesis quirk rather than a bug. Low priority.

critical_code_review_comments: Single ~525-line static HTML/CSS/JS file is acceptable for the deliverable scope (GitHub Pages). No backend, no auth, no APIs — matches spec.
All required data-testid attributes present and correctly wired.
Footer year is hardcoded '2026'; if intent was dynamic via JS new Date().getFullYear(), confirm — current static value happens to match Jan 2026 so tests pass.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure static page served from /app/frontend/public/index.html via the React shell (App.js returns null, index.css cleared). All 14 functional checks pass on desktop (1920) and mobile (375). Only nuance: programmatic .hover() in Playwright did not trigger transform translateY(-5px) on engineering cards in this run — verify visually in a real browser if needed. The /app/site/index.html mirror was not modified.
